cratic party proposes the vigorous
enforcement of the criminal law
against trust officials, and it pro-
poses legislation that will clearly
define that which is legitimate and
that which is unlawful. We draw
the line between the legitimate cor
poration which serves a useful pur-
pose, as nearly all of them do, and
which are regulated by competition,
and those illegitimate combines
which aspire to control market by
means of monopoly,


On the labor question we propose
certain definite reforms; first, that
a department of labor shall be es-,
tablished, with a cabinet officer at
it's head; second, that associations
of laboring men, of afrmers and
other similar associations shall be
taken out from under the operation
of the antitrust law; third, that a
labor dispute shall not in itself, be
a ground for injunction. and that
before injunction shall issue in
labor dispute something must be
done or Threatened that would JUS
tify an injunction even If there
were no labor dispute; fourth, that
trial by jury shall be permitted in
cases of direct contempt. The DEM
ocratic party precents these propo-
sitions and the republican party
opposes them.


The democratic party is in favor
of legislation which will ensure de-
positors against loss. At present
the banks are reasonably but not
absolutely secure. The federal gov
eminent, the state government, the
county government, the city and
the villages all require security
when they deposit money in the
banks. and we contend that the
ordinary depositor, who sometimes
entrusts all his savings to bank,
should have absolute security and
be protected from any possible loss.


We believe that the Filipinos
should be given a promise of ulti-
mate independence, in order that
they may be inspired to greater
earnestness and activity in the de-
velopment of all that goes to inn
prove their country. Imperialism
not only involves us in enormous
expense, but it surrenders our
ideals of government and paralyzes
our influence as the leader in the
progress toward constitutional gov.
eminent.


There are other issues, bet these
are presented as the more promi-
nent ones.
